data.title: Aussie Broadband rises on upbeat results, outlook upgrade
data.description:
** Aussie Broadband rises 4.2% to A$3.990

** Stock earlier rose to A$4.290, highest since March 13, 2024

** Internet service provider posts HY NPAT of A$12.2 million ($7.77 million), up 23.8% y/y

** Upgrades underlying EBITDA outlook to A$133 mln-A$138 mln from earlier A$125 mln-A$135 mln range

** Raises FY capex outlook by A$20 million

** UBS positive on FY guidance upgrade and continuance of market share gains across every segment, which looks to have continued through Q3 FY25

** Citi says ABB's outlook uplift and reiteration of its commitment to reach 100,000 subscribers for Buddy in next 2-3 years illustrate Group's confidence in its growth trajectory

** Stock up 11.5% YTD

($1 = A$1.5699)

data.title: Nissan offers suitors daunting risk-reward trade
data.description:
KKR has joined Foxconn in mulling an investment, and some Japanese bigwigs may want to involve Tesla. CEO Makoto Uchida’s turnaround plan could roughly triple the carmaker’s valuation. But it means cutting sticky costs, halting sliding sales and hiking prices. It’s a tall order.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @KatrinaHamlin on X

data.title: PolyNovo Posts Higher Fiscal H1 Earnings as Revenue Rises
data.description:
PolyNovo reported Monday fiscal half-year diluted earnings of AU$0.0048 per share, up from AU$0.0039 per share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected diluted EPS of AU$0.005.

Total revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$59.9 million, up from AU$48.8 million a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected AU$62.1 million.

The board did not declare a dividend for the period.

The company's shares fell past 2% in recent Monday trade.

data.title: Delta Electronics Unit Reappoints Chairman, Vice Chairman
data.description:
Delta Electronics unit Cyntec reappointed Steven Liu as chairman and Shou-Fong Chin as vice-chairman of the company on Friday.

The appointments were made following the expiration of their previous terms, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: India's Sensex Opens 0.55% Lower at 74893.45
Australia's Perenti slides on lower half-yearly profit
data.description:
** Shares of Perenti down 16.5% at A$1.1525

** Stock falls as much as 19.9% earlier in the day to A$1.105, posting its biggest intraday pct drop since mid-May 2021

** Mining services provider reports statutory NPAT of A$63.5 mln ($40.52 mln) for 1H25 vs A$69.8 mln for the same period last year

** Says lower profit due to one-off costs, including product development costs

** More than 6 mln shares change hands, 2.7 times the 30-day avg

** Stock down ~17.6% YTD, including current session's moves

($1 = 1.5672 Australian dollars)

data.title: Australian tech stocks hit 3-1/2-month low, track Wall Street peers
data.description:
** Australian tech stocks fall as much as 8.8% to 3-1/2-month low in its steepest drop since mid-May 2022

** Last down 7%

** Stocks set to log fourth straight session of losses

** Wall Street peers fell on Friday on weak economic data and worries of softening consumer demand

** Adding pressure, shares of Australian logistics software maker WiseTech Global slid nearly 22% after four directors stepped down due to differing views around the role of the company's billionaire founder and former CEO Richard White

** Tech sub-index has declined 3.4% so far this year

data.title: Federal Bank shares in focus as brokerages give thumps up to its roadmap for next 3 years
data.description:
Shares of Federal Bank will gain traction on February 24 after the private lender underlined 12 different themes to ratchet up bank's return on asset (RoA) to top 6 banks by FY28 at its strategy meet. Brokerages have retained their bullish ratings on the counter, seeing up to 35 percent potential upside.

Over the past 3 months, shares of Federal Bank have tumbled 13 percent, as against 4 percent drop in the benchmark Nifty 50 index.

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

Analysts at Citi reiterated its "buy" rating on Federal Bank and put out highest target price of Rs 242. New CEO KVS Manian unveiled "Breakthrough phase" strategy at its meet, which aims to modernise and position the bank closer to top private lenders with a universal bank vision.

"Valuations at 1.1x FY27E book appear reasonable for Federal Bank. The focus is on enhancing profitability or RoA while sustaining growth. The management aims to rationalise cost of deposits, reshape product mix, and enhance fees," added the brokerage firm.

IIFL Securities too upgraded the counter to "buy" and upped target price to Rs 218 from Rs 185 per share. Analysts see risk-reward favorable for Federal Bank after 17 percent correction in stock price.

"The bank aspires to become a PAN-India and truly universal bank. The focus is on profitability over growth and aggressive targets have been laid out," noted IIFL Securities.

Meanwhile, Nuvama Institutional Equities maintained its bullish call on Federal Bank and shared target price of Rs 215 apiece.

"At its strategy meet, the CEO noted 12 themes to ratchet up the bank's RoA to the level of top 6 banks by FY28. Improving share of CA by 4 percent would be the top focus. New products shall be launched with special focus on wealth affordable housing, used CVs and tractors," they added.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea, Japan Partner on Hydrogen Ecosystem Development
data.description:
South Korea and Japan are ramping up efforts to establish a global hydrogen ecosystem through joint public and private sector initiatives, Pulse News reported Friday.

Hyundai Motor Group, the parent company of Hyundai Motor and Kia , and Japan's Toyota Motor , will support a bilateral cooperation event from March 10 to March 12, aimed at fostering hydrogen industry collaboration, the report said, citing industry sources.

Shares of Hyundai Motor and Kia fell nearly 1% in recent trade on Monday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Singapore Dollar Strengthens Amid Risk-On Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session, driven by risk-on sentiment fueled by gains in U.S. stock-index futures and projected results from Germany's election. Initial estimates showed the CDU and its CSU sister party securing just under 29% of the vote, followed by Alternative for Germany, or AfD, with around 20%. Given that the election outcome aligns with expectations, analysts at Maybank say there are hopes of reduced political uncertainty. Additionally, signs of fading U.S. exceptionalism have emerged, supported by recent economic data. USD/SGD is 0.3% lower at 1.3319. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: ASE Technology Unit to Buy Equipment for 200 Million Yuan; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
ASE Technology's unit, SPIL (Suzhou), plans to acquire equipment from its affiliate, ISE Labs, China, for 199.7 million yuan.

The acquisition will optimize the group's resource allocation, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Both the aforementioned entities are owned by ASE Technology.

ASE Technology shares fell 3% in late-morning trading Monday.

data.title: Powertech Technology Board OKs Up to NT$5 Billion Debt Issue
data.description:
Powertech Technology's board approved a plan to issue domestic or overseas corporate or convertible bonds, with a total limit of NT$5 billion or 75.9 million common shares.

The bonds will have a term of up to seven years, according to a Monday Taiwan Exchange filing. The coupon rate and other terms will be determined later.

Funds raised will support advanced assembly and testing equipment, R&D, working capital, and potential IC backend technology partnerships, the company said.

data.title: Millennium & Copthorne Hotels Posts Lower 2024 Earnings Despite Rise in Revenue; Shares Hit Five-Year High
data.description:
Millennium & Copthorne Hotels New Zealand reported Monday 2024 diluted earnings of NZ$0.0175 per share, down from NZ$0.1365 per share a year earlier.

Revenue for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was NZ$176.2 million, up from NZ$145.7 million a year earlier.

The board did not declare a dividend for the period as part of the conditions under CDL Hotels Holdings New Zealand's takeover bid for the company.

The Kiwi hotel operator's shares were up nearly 6% in recent Monday trade, earlier hitting their highest since January 2020.

data.title: Marlin Global Post Lower Fiscal H1 Earnings, Revenue
data.description:
Marlin Global reported Monday that its earnings per diluted share in the fiscal first half fell to NZ$0.037, from NZ$0.049 per diluted share a year earlier.

Total income for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was NZ$11.3 million, down 9% from NZ$12.4 million in the same period last year.

The board declared an interim dividend of NZ$0.0205 per share, up from the $0.0186 in the previous reported period, payable on March 28 to shareholders on record as of March 6.

data.title: China Southern Airlines Subsidiary Withdraws IPO Application
data.description:
China Southern Airlines subsidiary China Southern Airlines Logistics withdrew its application for a mainboard listing on the Shanghai Stock Exchange due to changing market conditions.

The company said the decision will not impact its operations, financial health or strategic plans, according to a Feb. 21 filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of China Southern Airlines rose 2% in recent trade on the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: King Yuan Electronics Buys Machinery Equipment Worth NT$1.27 Billion
data.description:
King Yuan Electronics acquired 133 sets of machinery equipment for IC testing from Hon. Precision (TPE:7769) for NT$1.27 billion.

The average unit price was NT$9.56 million, according to a Friday Taiwan Exchange filing.

data.title: Bank of England speakers Monday: Deputy Governors Lombardelli & Ramsden plus Dhingra
data.description:
The times below are in GMT/US Eastern time format:

0900/0400 Bank of England Deputy Governor Clare Lombardelli gives opening remarks at BoE research conference

  • Lombardelli was the OECD's Chief Economist and G20 Finance Deputy since May 2023
  • was appointed Deputy Governor for Monetary Policy (DGMP), to succeed Ben Broadbent from 1 July 2024

1315/0815 Bank of England Deputy Governor Dave Ramsden chairs discussion on central bank balance sheets

1800/1300 Bank of England Monetary Policy Committee external member Swati Dhingra gives a speech on the state of UK monetary policy at London's Birkbeck University

data.title: Shareholders that lost money on Integral Ad Science Holding Corp.(IAS) Urged to Join Class Action - Contact Levi & Korsinsky to Learn More
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 23, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Integral Ad Science Holding Corp. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/integral-ad-science-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131681&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Integral Ad Science Holding Corp. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between March 2, 2023 and February 27, 2024.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) IAS was experiencing a new material trend of increased competitive pricing pressures and as a result, IAS had been forced to cut prices to compensate for weakening demand and slowing revenue growth; (ii) IAS's pricing function was no longer "favorable" and IAS could not sustain its pricing and drive price increases; (iii) pricing had become a key differentiator between IAS and its competitor necessary to close major renewals and new deals; (iv) the risks that competition "could result in increased pricing pressure" or "could put pressure on us to change our prices" had in fact transpired; and (v) as a result, the IAS's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Integral Ad Science stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/integral-ad-science-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=131681&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Kiwi Dollar Rises to Over 2-Month High
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar rose to around $0.577 on Monday, recouping losses from the previous session to trade at more than two-month high after domestic data showed strong retail sales.

New Zealand’s retail trade increased 0.9% quarter-on-quarter in Q4 2024, following a revised flat reading in the previous period, the largest gain in three years.

This supported expectations of a slower pace of rate cuts, aligning with the RBNZ’s signal last week that future reductions would likely be smaller and that the easing cycle was nearing its end.

Additionally, China released its 2025 policy statement, focusing on rural reforms and revitalization.

Optimism about its stimulus measures supports the Kiwi dollar, given China’s trade ties with New Zealand.

Externally, a weaker US dollar further boosts the NZD, as soft US data raises concerns about the economy, potentially prompting the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates further.

data.title: Japanese Yen Holds Steady in Holiday Trade
data.description:
The Japanese yen traded around 149.3 per dollar on Monday, holding steady at 12-week highs as domestic markets were closed for a public holiday.

The yen had rallied sharply last week, supported by stronger-than-expected inflation data in Japan, which reinforced expectations for further interest rate hikes by the Bank of Japan.

BOJ officials also signaled their intention to adjust policy if economic conditions continue to evolve as anticipated.

However, BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda noted that the central bank is ready to act swiftly in response to market shifts, including through market operations if abnormal movements occur.

Ueda’s comments came as Japan’s 10-year government bond yield surged to its highest level in over 15 years.

Externally, the dollar weakened broadly amid growing concerns about the US economy.

data.title: NetEase's Online Gaming Revenue Likely to Recover in 2025 — Market Talk
data.description: NetEase's online gaming revenue will likely recover this year, Nomura analysts say in a research note. The resumption of Blizzard-licensed titles and the release of new hit titles should support the Chinese company's gaming revenue in 2025. the analysts say. Nomura expects NetEase's deferred game revenue to resume growth in 1Q, after dropping 2.5% on quarter in 4Q likely due to seasonality and a high base. Meanwhile, the Nomura analysts think the NetEase stock can act as "an appealing portfolio stabilizer" during market volatility, as its online gaming business is unlikely to be affected by either macro weakness or geopolitical turmoil. Nomura keeps a buy call on NetEase's ADRs and raises its target price slightly to $124.00 from $123.00. Shares last closed at $103.22. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: NetEase ADR Target Price Raised to $124.00 From $123.00 by Nomura >NTES
Offshore Yuan Rises Amid Dollar Weakness
data.description:
The offshore yuan strengthened around 7.23 per dollar, supported by a weakening US dollar amid concerns about a slowing US economy and persistent inflation, which could prompt the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates.

Investor sentiment was further bolstered by Alibaba's announcement to invest over $52 billion in AI and cloud computing over the next three years, signaling confidence in China's tech sector.

Additionally, China’s 2025 rural revitalization plan, focused on agricultural reforms and economic growth in rural areas, added to the positive outlook for the yuan.

On the monetary side, the PBOC kept its key lending rates unchanged in February 2025, with the one-year loan prime rate at 3.1% and the five-year rate at 3.6%.

However, further gains were limited by renewed U.S.-China trade tensions, as U.S. President Trump reportedly instructed the Committee on Foreign Investment to restrict Chinese investments in sectors like energy and other critical industries.

data.title: Australian Dollar Rises as Greenback Weakens
data.description:
The Australian dollar gained 0.5% to around $0.638 on Monday, recovering losses from the previous session as the greenback came under pressure due to a weakening economic outlook in the US and a stronger euro.

The Aussie also tracked the Chinese yuan higher after top officials from Washington and Beijing held a phone call on Friday, sparking hopes for a potential trade deal.

Domestically, investors are looking ahead to Australia’s monthly CPI data this week, which could provide guidance on the outlook for monetary policy.

Last week, the Reserve Bank of Australia lowered its cash rate by 25 basis points to 4.1%, as expected, but signaled a more cautious approach to further rate cuts, citing concerns that the disinflation process could stall.

In a press conference following the meeting, RBA Governor Michele Bullock emphasized that the central bank cannot yet declare victory over inflation and warned there may be limited room for additional cuts.

data.title: Ping An Bank Approves 6.7 Billion Yuan Offshore Deposit Transaction With Lufax
data.description:
Ping An Bank's board has approved a related-party transaction with financial services provider Lufax , involving offshore time deposits, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

The total principal amount for the time deposit business will not exceed 4.50 billion yuan, with total interest capped at 29 million yuan.

The agreement deposit business will have a principal amount not exceeding the equivalent of 2.18 billion yuan, and interest not exceeding 18 million yuan.

Both Ping An Bank and Lufax are subsidiaries of Ping An Insurance (Group) .

data.title: Spark NZ Price Target Cut 6.6% to NZ$4.25/Share by UBS
